payload.answer: The source says AI memory systems should keep durable source evidence; atomic memory cards should stay small and traceable; and while a semantic index may help recall, the application database should own lifecycle and conflict state.
citations: For context, AI memory systems need durable source evidence.
Atomic memory cards should be small and traceable.
A semantic index can improve recall, but the application database must own lifecycle and conflict state.
payload.decision: Commit the source-backed design principles as tentative memory cards with low-to-moderate specificity, preserving the distinction between recall support and lifecycle ownership.

entity_resolution.reason: No existing entity identifier was provided; the content is general guidance rather than a reference to a known persistent entity.

payload.intent: Extract durable, source-traceable takeaways about AI memory system design from the provided context.

memory_cards.statement: AI memory systems need durable source evidence for stored memories.
Atomic memory cards should be small and traceable.
A semantic index can improve recall, but the application database must own lifecycle and conflict state.

details: Preserved ambiguity by not inferring any additional system design details beyond the text.
Avoided promoting the semantic index to system-of-record status.

repair_options: Store the three statements as separate atomic cards.
Attach source text or provenance metadata to each card.
Treat the semantic index as a retrieval aid, not the source of lifecycle/conflict truth.
